title: epipactis atrorubens subvar. lutescens
intro: Epipactis atrorubens subvar. lutescens, also known as the yellow-tinged helleborine, is a rare and lesser-known orchid species native to Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to act as a diuretic and to treat urinary issues due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other compounds. The health benefits of Epipactis atrorubens subvar. lutescens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the roots, which are then used in infusions or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
